# development-designer Experience

## Purpose

Persistent implementation notes for generating and maintaining {{proj}} source design documents.

## Current Design Contract

- Maintain `.design.json` files under every `src/` folder.
- The `design-json-update` skill owns the mechanics of selecting and updating `.design.json` files. `development-designer` should use that skill when the hook provides changed source paths.
- JSON is the only source-design companion metadata format going forward.
- Every dot-prefixed file or folder under `src/` is companion metadata owned by development-designer. Maintain these paths beside source files, but never list them as normal source files, child modules, or source change entries.
- `.design.json` content should be machine-readable JSON.
- All paths should be repository-rooted and under `src/`.
- Do not list dot-prefixed files or folders as child modules/files.
- Keep design documents concise and source-focused.

## Extraction Rules

- Treat every folder under `src/` as a module.
- Describe direct child modules and direct child source files.
- List meaningful interfaces, traits, structs, enums, impl blocks, functions, type aliases, constants, statics, and data structures as top-level `elements`.
- Every element should include `name`, `type`, `changeStatus`, and `codeSegments`.
- Do not store signatures or copied source code in metadata.
- `codeSegments` should point to implementation file and line ranges; one item may have multiple segments, such as a struct plus its impl blocks.

## Elements

- `elements` lists public source definitions that downstream consumers may display.
- Include only concrete public definitions owned by the module layer.
- Do not include wiring declarations such as `mod ...`, `pub mod ...`, or `pub use ...` in `elements`.
- Do not include Cargo manifests or package metadata as `elements`; they can inform module purpose but are not source elements.
- Do not expose single-field tuple wrappers such as `pub struct ModuleId(pub String);` unless they have meaningful behavior beyond wrapping.
- Create one exposed element per public definition; never combine names with `/`, commas, or summary labels.
- Use concise `type` values such as `trait`, `struct`, `enum`, `type-alias`, `const`, `static`, or `function`.
- For struct elements, keep the struct as the primary metadata unit and list related impl/method implementation ranges in `codeSegments`; avoid scattering every impl method unless it is a standalone public function/API.

## Status Rules

- Include `changeStatus` when known on modules and elements.
- Use top-level status arrays (`added`, `modified`, `deleted`, `renamed`) for the current folder and its direct files only. Use `"."` for the current folder itself; do not put `changeStatus` on `childModules`.
- Valid statuses are `unchanged`, `added`, `modified`, `deleted`, and `renamed`.
- Prefer explicit item-level `changeStatus`.
- Status values should reflect source changes, not metadata generation side effects.
- `development-designer` should be triggered only by the `ensure-deveopment-design` hook, not proactively during normal source-editing tasks.
- For every changed design-tracked source path under `src/`, update `.design.json` in that file's folder and every ancestor folder up to `src/`.
- The `ensure-deveopment-design` `agentStop` hook checks only design-tracked `src/` files written by the current agent turn and blocks completion if their ancestor `.design.json` files were not also updated.
- When explicit tag comparison data is available, use it as evidence. Otherwise update statuses from the current task's source changes and preserve unaffected definitions as `unchanged`.
- Element status is more precise than file status: a modified file can contain unchanged elements. Do not mark every element in a changed file as `modified`; only mark elements whose own definition, related impl blocks, public API, behavior, or owned source location changed.

## Update Workflow

- When source structure or public interfaces change, update the design document in the affected `src/` folder.
- Keep module-level purpose and element definitions aligned.
- Keep the JSON payload schema-compatible and machine-readable without custom prose parsing.

## Lessons

- 2026-06-19: Do not mark current source definitions `added` merely because design metadata is regenerated; when previous-tag comparison data is unavailable, default checked-in source modules and elements to `unchanged` until a real add/modify/delete/rename is known.
- 2026-06-20: Design refresh is enforced by the `ensure-deveopment-design` hook, not by proactive calls during normal tasks or by `git-sync`. When the hook blocks, invoke `development-designer` with the current-agent changed paths/sections so `.design.json` stays in sync before task completion.
- 2026-06-19: When the Rust crate root lives under `src`, Cargo metadata may inform root module purpose, but `src/Cargo.toml` itself is not an element. Keep Cargo dot-prefixed configuration hidden as companion metadata.
- 2026-06-21: For focused implementation-signature changes such as `src/core/preprocess/preprocessor.rs` switching to `InputChatMessage`, mark the owning element and direct source file `modified`; ancestor design files should not list descendant paths.
- 2026-06-21: A direct source file listed in `.design.json` `modified` does not imply every element in that file is modified. Preserve elements such as unchanged request/response structs as `unchanged` when their `codeSegments` text did not change.
- 2026-06-25: Treat `src/tests/` as integration-test code outside source-design metadata. Do not create `src/tests/.design.json`, list it as a child module, or require design updates for files under it.
- 2026-06-27: For engine/frontdoor API redrafts, exclude module wiring elements (`pub mod`, `pub use`) and root Cargo/config metadata; mark ancestor folders with `modified: ["."]` rather than listing changed child folders. Source: ensure-deveopment-design focused update for `src/agent/engine` and `src/agent/frontdoor`.
- 2026-06-27: When a Rust module changes from sibling file-plus-folder (`message.rs` plus `message/`) to folder-only (`message/mod.rs`), remove parent module-file elements, mark the deleted parent file in the parent `.design.json`, and move current public elements to the folder module metadata without listing module wiring. Source: focused message module layout update.
- 2026-06-27: When a correction splits a folder module entry from concrete definitions, keep `mod.rs` wiring-only in metadata, point elements at the concrete leaf file, and remove deleted aliases such as `TaskRuntimeReceiver` from current elements. Source: focused pre-tag correction for `src/common/message` and `src/agent/engine`.
- 2026-06-27: For style-only Rust refactors that move top-level helper functions into a main struct's private impl without changing crate-visible APIs, mark the owning struct/impl element and direct source file `modified`, update current line ranges, and keep sibling elements unchanged. Source: focused `src/agent/engine/loop_engine.rs` LoopEngine helper-method refactor.
- 2026-06-27: For hosted task-context frontdoor changes, mark only the owning task element lines that changed (for example `AgentTask::run` creating/returning `TaskContext`) and clear stale highlights on unchanged transport methods such as `send`, `receive`, and `complete`. Source: focused `src/agent/frontdoor/task.rs` update.
- 2026-06-27: For refactors that make an engine/session context cloneable to keep private helpers as `&self` methods, record the derive line as an added segment on the owning struct, mark the method/impl segment modified, and list only the direct changed files in the module status array. Source: focused `src/agent/engine/{loop_engine.rs,session_context.rs}` update.
- 2026-06-30: For new public child modules exposed by a parent `mod.rs`, keep the parent childModules entry status-free, mark the direct parent `mod.rs` as modified, and put `added: ["."]` in the new child module's own `.design.json`; for config-gated fields, highlight only the owning public struct's added field lines. Source: focused common logging module update.
- 2026-06-30: For logging shortcut API additions, record each new public shortcut function as its own `added` element in the owning logger file, keep re-export-only `mod.rs` changes out of `elements`, and leave moved private statics/constants out of selectable metadata. Source: focused common logging warning/error API update.
- 2026-06-30: For private runtime-path or log-directory behavior changes, keep private helpers out of `elements` and attach their line ranges to the affected public loading/logging element; for a new native catalog child module, mark the child module `added: ["."]` and each public enum/struct/const catalog item as `added` while leaving parent re-export wiring out of elements. Source: focused update for `src/common/config`, `src/common/logging`, and `src/client/tool/native`.
- 2026-06-30: When native tool metadata moves from manager/catalog files to folder-per-category implementation modules, keep parent `childModules` status-free, mark deleted direct files in the parent `deleted` array, and model each concrete native tool struct with segments for the struct, associated `DEFINITION`, `ToolClassification`, `Tool`, and `NativeTool` impls. - 2026-07-01: When a Tool method changes return type to a shared protocol struct (Tool::schema() -> common::protocol ToolSchema) and each native tool swaps a single `pub const PREVIEW: ToolPreview` for `pub const NAME/DESCRIPTION/SCHEMA` in an inherent impl, only the const block above the `impl Tool` block changes line count; shift every downstream codeSegment by that const-block delta, set modifiedLines to just the changed NAME..SCHEMA and the name()/description()/schema()/invoke-param lines, and clear (position-shift only) fossil highlights on untouched trailing helper segments. - 2026-07-01: `ToolInvocation.arguments: String` -> `parameter: ToolParameter` and `ToolPreview.schema: &'static str` -> `ToolSchema` are same-line-count edits, so mark only those field lines modified and keep the rest of the struct/impl unchanged; ToolPreview losing Copy is a derive-line change, not a new element. - 2026-07-01: For a brand-new single-owner shared module used by two consumers (src/common/protocol owning ToolSchema/ToolParameter), model the module changeStatus "added" with top-level added:["."], list each struct definition + its inherent impl as separate codeSegments, and register it as a childModule (no changeStatus) in the parent. Reference the shared owner from consumers only at purpose/workflow level unless a consumer element concretely names the type â€” do not fabricate element-level protocol usage in agent/engine when its listed elements (Plan/Job/TaskStep) do not import the protocol types. Source: common::protocol shared tool schema/parameter introduction.
- 2026-07-01: Ancestor rollups whose only real change is in descendants use module.changeStatus "modified" + modified:["."] (never list descendant folder paths in top-level arrays), and communicate cross-module additions purely by editing the affected childModule purpose text and the module purpose (e.g. "client::tool sources ToolSchema/ToolParameter from common::protocol"; engine "model-produced Plan/Job planning"). When the ancestor's own mod.rs actually changed, list the real filename instead of ".". Source: src/agent, src/client, src rollups for common::protocol + engine planning.

- 2026-07-02: For interrupted flat-to-folder task rewrites, create the missing folder module metadata with `added:["."]`, keep parent and root rollups at `modified:["."]`, leave `mod.rs`/`lib.rs` wiring out of elements, and document panic-backed methods only as interface placeholders. Source: focused `src/agent/task` design completion. claiming implemented behavior. Source: focused `src/common/structure` WorkQueue interface draft.
- 2026-07-03: For crate-root ownership-only import corrections in restored common modules, mark the directly changed file in its own metadata and ancestor folders as modified while leaving public elements unchanged and clearing stale element highlights when changed lines are only `use` paths outside selectable definitions. - 2026-07-03: For fmt-only restored external dependency re-export wrappers, mark the directly changed wrapper files in the owning module metadata, leave re-export-only files out of `elements`, keep ancestors at `modified:["."]`, and skip workflow edits when responsibilities/flow are unchanged. Source: focused update for `src/common/external/{reqwest.rs,serde_json.rs}`.
- 2026-07-04: For a FULL pre-tag refresh of a flat-crate rewrite (common/agent/client/host with `lib.rs` roots), treat brand-new folder modules (structure, session, task, model, host, client, protocol) as `added:["."]` with every element `added` even when the folder path pre-existed, provided all its current files are new/rewritten; do NOT also list the deleted old files/subtrees in an `added` module â€” absence from childModules/elements is sufficient and avoids the `added`-module-listing-deletions contradiction. Record crate-root migration (`mod.rs`â†’`lib.rs`) as `added: ["lib.rs"]` + `deleted: ["mod.rs"/"main.rs"]` on the affected roots, kept at module `modified:["."]`. Source: full pre-tag refresh of `src/**`.
- 2026-07-04: During a full refresh, do not trust prior "verified accurate" notes for `.workflow.mmd` â€” stale diagrams silently retain deleted-module nodes (e.g. config workflow kept `ToolRegistry`/`NativeTools`/`agent main`/`cli run`; external workflow kept `client/core`/`agent/frontdoor`/`common/message`). Re-derive every diagram's consumer nodes from actual current `use`/reference grep, and add a `%%` note for real-but-unwired wrapper files (reqwest/serde_json/image reserved for stubbed backends) instead of drawing fake edges. Guard the refresh with a banned-token scan across ALL design+workflow files for the deleted-module names. Source: config/external workflow rewrites during full pre-tag refresh.
- 2026-07-04: A uniform field insertion (e.g. `AgentConfig.max_turns`) shifts every subsequent element's `codeSegments` by a fixed +1; verify each element's start line lands on its `#[derive(...)]`/`pub` keyword and its end line on the closing `}`/`;` with an automated line-range check (watch for off-by-one at end lines and out-of-range past EOF), and keep elements `unchanged` when only their location moved. Source: `src/common/config/config.rs` +1 shift, DeepseekConfig Debug impl end 425â†’424 fix.
- 2026-07-04: The `ensure-deveopment-design` hook only blocks on NON-dot source paths the agent itself wrote (transcript `*** Add/Update/Delete/Move` patch markers, filtered by `Test-NonDotSourcePath`); since development-designer writes only dot-prefixed `.design.json`/`.workflow.mmd`, its `relevantPaths` is always empty and the hook returns `allow` â€” so completion is safe regardless of the pre-existing `.rs` rewrite, which was authored outside this agent's turn. Source: `.github/hooks/ensure-deveopment-design.ps1`.

## 2026-07-03
- Removing a trait method (e.g. ModelBackendImpl::ready) that only shifts line ranges: mark the owning trait element and its implementors (DeepseekBackend impl segment) as `modified` with modifiedLines on the changed span; leave sibling elements `unchanged` with empty highlight arrays and refreshed line ranges.
- The ensure-deveopment-design hook only requires each ancestor .design.json to appear in git changes; when a parent folder has no elements owning the file, make a meaningful source-backed childModule/module purpose edit so the file is genuinely changed. The hook does not inspect .workflow.mmd.

## 2026-07-03 (visibility promotion + behavior fill-in)
- Visibility-only promotion (`pub(crate)`/`pub(super)` -> `pub`) on external re-export wrappers: mark the module + every changed wrapper file `modified` (list all direct filenames in the `modified` array), and for a wrapper that owns a real element (e.g. `connect_remoc`), keep the same line range and set `modifiedLines` to just the signature/visibility line (line 5). Re-export-only wrapper files (reqwest/serde/serde_json/tokio/toml/image mod.rs) stay out of `elements`; they are tracked only via the `modified` array. The parent `common` rollup lists the real changed file (`lib.rs` â€” `pub mod external`) not `.`.
- "Behavior filled in" on a previously panic-backed backend: refresh completely-stale line ranges from the current source (backend_deepseek.rs went from ~31 lines to 166). Use `addedLines` for net-new implementation bodies (send body, whole new private-helper impl block `stream_response`/`take_next_sse_event`/`handle_sse_event`) and for newly added `From`/`Display` impl blocks on an error enum; use `modifiedLines` for in-place changed spans (struct decl+derive gaining fields, `new()` body loading config, a Debug impl body now printing fields, a blanket `impl` body switching `panic!` -> `self.send(request)`). The enum/struct definition line itself stays highlight-free when only its impls changed; the element's overall `changeStatus` becomes `modified`.
- Attach private helpers and every related `impl` block (`From<io/serde_json/reqwest::Error>`, `Display`, marker `std::error::Error {}`) as separate `codeSegments` on the owning enum/struct element rather than inventing private elements; a marker impl not in the caller's change list stays an `unchanged` segment.

## 2026-07-05 (cross-crate type relocation: tool subsystem)
- When a public type MOVES between crates/modules, the correct changeStatus is per-owner: `deleted` from the old owner (conveyed by ABSENCE â€” drop the module from `childModules` / the element from `elements`, never fabricate a `deleted`-folder entry) and `added` in the NEW owning file (even if the type's body is byte-identical to before). Example: `ToolPreview`/`ToolSchema` moved from the deleted `common/tool/` into new `common/protocol/tool.rs` â†’ both are `added` in `common/protocol/.design.json`; the `Tool` executable handle moved from `common/tool/tool.rs` into `host/tool/tool.rs` â†’ `added` in `host/tool`; the pre-existing host `ToolProgram` trait was renamed `tool.rs`â†’`tool_program.rs` (content unchanged) â†’ new file listed in module `added:["tool_program.rs"]` with the trait element `added`.
- ALWAYS run `git diff HEAD` on each touched `.rs` before assigning line ranges/status â€” do NOT trust the caller's prose about "only the import changed". In this task the caller framed it as an import-only move, but the diff showed `execution.rs`/`execution_context.rs` were full panic-stubâ†’implementation rewrites carried in the SAME uncommitted worktree from an earlier window. The metadata must reflect the FULL diff vs the last reset commit, regardless of which logical task introduced each hunk.
- Two OPPOSITE line shifts can occur in one refresh from import-line count changes: consolidating two `use` lines into one (`use crate::protocol::{ExeId,TaskId};` + `use crate::tool::ToolPreview;` â†’ one line) shifts everything below by âˆ’1 (`common/protocol/execution.rs`); splitting one import into two (adding `use marix_host_tool::Tool;`) shifts everything below by +1 (`host/executor/execution/*`). Recompute every codeSegment start (`#[derive]`/`pub` keyword) and end (closing `}`/`;`) and every addedLines/modifiedLines range by the file's own delta, then bounds-check against current source. A type whose definition only moved lines but is textually unchanged stays `unchanged` with cleared highlight fossils; only the element whose field actually changed (`ExecutionParameterPackage.signature`) stays `modified` with a tight modifiedLines on that field.
- A referenced-but-unchanged type is NOT a modification: `ExecutionSessionEvent::Preview { tools: Vec<ToolPreview> }` stayed `unchanged` even though `ToolPreview` now resolves from a different module path â€” the enum's own source text is identical, so only the `use` line above it (outside any element segment) changed.
- Manifest edits (`host/Cargo.toml` gaining `marix-host-tool = { path = "tool" }`, `Cargo.lock`) are never elements or `modified`-array entries; note the new dependency in module purpose text only. They still force the ancestor `.design.json` chain to appear in `git diff --name-only HEAD`, which is the real ancestor-chain correctness check.
- Workflow relabel discipline for a moved owner: change the subgraph id+label (`common_tool[common tool]` â†’ `common_protocol[common protocol]` or `host_tool[host tool crate marix-host-tool]`) but KEEP the node id (`Tool`, `ToolPreview`) so existing edges stay valid; move the node into the correct crate's subgraph and delete the emptied subgraph. Re-point ownership edges to the true holder (e.g. `ExecutionContext -->|holds Tool handle| Tool` instead of `Executor --> Tool`). Verification: subgraph/end balance per file + every edge endpoint declared (guard against regex false positives from `\n--` inside quoted labels), plus a banned-token scan (`common_tool`,`common tool`,`common::tool`,`common/tool`,`ToolModule`) across all design+workflow files â€” a surviving hit is only acceptable inside intentional provenance prose like "relocated here from the removed common::tool module".
- Accumulation model: do NOT reset an element already `modified` from earlier in the same tag window (e.g. ModelBackendImpl) back to `unchanged` when the current task doesn't touch it â€” statuses persist until design-json-reset runs at tag time. Presence of pre-existing `modified` arrays/elements confirms mid-window; preserve them and layer the new task's changes on top. Add the newly changed file (error.rs) to the module `modified` array.
- Ancestors whose own files did not change (agent, src) use module `modified` + `modified:["."]` plus a source-backed childModule/module purpose edit describing the descendant change (implemented Deepseek backend; now-public external adapters) so the file genuinely changes for the hook.
- Verify every segment start/end lands on its real definition boundary and every addedLines/modifiedLines range maps to the intended source text with a quick python line-dump before finishing. Source: focused update for src/common/external, src/common, src/agent/model, src/agent, src.
- 2026-07-03: When a stubbed struct runtime is implemented and shared state is extracted into a new sibling context struct (Task -> Task + TaskContext): mark the new file `added:["file.rs"]` with the context struct `added` (struct + inherent impl segments fully green); mark the owner file `modified` with the owner struct `modified` â€” modifiedLines on just the replaced field span (field set changed) and the filled-in public method bodies, and addedLines on the brand-new private `impl` block (helpers were absent in the stub, so they are added not modified). List the changed wiring `mod.rs` in the owning folder's `modified` array even though it stays out of `elements`. Sibling files (step.rs/execution.rs) whose lines did not move stay `unchanged`, untouched. For the parent whose own `lib.rs` re-export actually changed this window, switch its top-level array from a descendant `"."` rollup to the real filename (`modified:["lib.rs"]`); the grandparent with no own-file change stays `modified:["."]` and communicates the change via childModule/module purpose edits. Move shared-state ownership edges (WorkQueue/backend/senders/signature) from the owner node to the context node in `.workflow.mmd`, leaving the owner node as the runtime driver (model request/response, client status stream, host event forward). Source: focused update for src/agent/task (TaskContext extraction + Task runtime implementation), src/agent, src.
- 2026-07-03: For a pure public free-function rename with no signature/body change (`channel`->`build_channel`, `create_channel`->`connect_channel` in `src/common/structure/channel.rs`): rename the element `name` in place (do NOT keep the old name or add a second element), set its `changeStatus` to `modified`, keep the same `codeSegments` line range, and put `modifiedLines` on JUST the signature line while clearing fossil `addedLines`. Mark the owning module `modified` and list the real changed files (`modified: ["channel.rs", "mod.rs"]`) since the `pub use` re-export line in `mod.rs` also changed (wiring, not an element). Update the module purpose text and the `.workflow.mmd` node label + edge labels that spell the old names (`channel / accept_channel / create_channel` -> `build_channel / accept_channel / connect_channel`; `channel() returns local pair` -> `build_channel()`; `accept/create return remoc pair` -> `accept/connect`). Leave the untouched `accept_channel` element and all alias/enum/WorkQueue elements exactly as-is. Do NOT touch generic prose that merely contains the word "channel" (e.g. "channel endpoints", "channel helpers", "SessionEvent channel", `mpsc::channel()`, or `channel.rs` sourcePaths) â€” only the standalone function identifiers were renamed. Source: focused `src/common/structure` channel constructor rename.
- 2026-07-03: When a same-line-count rename's call sites live inside files already `modified`/`added` earlier in the same tag window (Task::new and DeepseekBackend::send now call `build_channel()`), no additional element/range edit is needed in `src/agent/task/.design.json` or `src/agent/model/.design.json`: the changed identifier sits within an already-`modified` element body and the rename shifts no lines, and those ancestor `.design.json` files are already in the git change set â€” so the hook is already satisfied for them. The only design file needing an edit was the rename's own folder, `src/common/structure/.design.json`, whose absence from the git-change set was the sole thing that would have blocked the hook (the hook only checks that each ancestor `.design.json` appears in git changes, not its contents). Source: focused channel-rename ancestor check for src/common/structure and call sites in agent task/model.

- 2026-07-03: Flat host module file promoted to a folder module (src/host/executor.rs -> src/host/executor/{mod.rs,executor.rs,execution.rs}). Verified via git ls-tree <lasttag> + git status: the whole src/host/executor/ folder is new (`??`) and executor.rs is `D`, so the folder is `added:["."]` in its own new .design.json with every moved/new public struct `added` (Executor struct+impl in executor.rs, ToolExecution struct in execution.rs, source_depth 3), and mod.rs `mod`/`pub use` re-export wiring stays OUT of elements. In the parent src/host/.design.json: DELETE the old direct-file elements that moved down (Executor/ToolProgram/ToolExecution were direct host elements; only HostSession from session.rs remains), ADD the executor childModule (no changeStatus), and record folder-level change with `deleted:["executor.rs"]` + `modified:["lib.rs"]` (the lib.rs re-export line dropped ToolProgram) at module `modified`. src rollup -> module `modified` + `modified:["."]`, communicate via host childModule purpose edit. When a type is removed (ToolProgram), run a banned-token scan across ALL src/**/.design.json + .workflow.mmd and strip it even from purpose text â€” do NOT keep "ToolProgram was removed" notes; purpose states current truth and removal is already conveyed by element absence + deleted array + tag diff. design-json-reset preserves codeSegments/addedLines and only clears changeStatus+status arrays, so an `unchanged` element legitimately keeps full addedLines (HostSession) â€” leave inherited unchanged elements verbatim. Hook only needs each ancestor .design.json for the changed paths present in the git change set; a brand-new folder's untracked .design.json satisfies it. Source: focused src/host executor flat-to-folder split (Executor now owns executions WorkQueue<ExeId,ToolExecution>).

- 2026-07-03: Public protocol type rename with identifier-only ripple (`ToolExecutionRequest`->`ExecutionRequest`, `ToolExecutionStatus`->`ExecutionStatus` in `src/common/protocol/execution.rs`), where source was already renamed in place and no lines shifted. In the rename's own `.design.json`: rename the element `name` in place (never keep the old name or add a duplicate), set `changeStatus: "modified"`, keep the identical `codeSegments` line range, clear the fossil full-range `addedLines` to `[]`, and set `modifiedLines` to JUST the renamed declaration line (struct/enum `pub` line). Mark referencing elements whose OWN definition lines changed text as `modified` too, with `modifiedLines` on the exact reference lines only (ExecutionSessionEvent variants `ExecutionEvoke(ExecutionRequest)`/`ExecutionStatus(ExecutionStatus)`; ExecutionParameterPackage `tool_request: Option<ExecutionRequest>`) â€” this is faithful, not over-propagation, because the enum/struct's own source text changed. Leave siblings whose lines are untouched `unchanged` with their inherited addedLines verbatim (ToolPreview, ExecutionSignature, and the genuinely-unchanged ToolExecutionUpdate whose name merely resembles the renamed ones). Module `modified` with `modified:["execution.rs","mod.rs"]` (the `pub use` re-export in mod.rs is wiring, listed via the array, not an element). Parent whose own re-export changed (`src/common/lib.rs`) -> module `modified` + `modified:["lib.rs"]`. Ripple-only child (`src/host/executor`) stays `added` this tag window â€” an added element/folder does NOT get downgraded to `modified` for an in-window identifier tweak; only fix its stale purpose text (`a ToolExecutionStatus` -> `an ExecutionStatus`). Run a banned-token scan across ALL `.design.json`+`.workflow.mmd` for the old names and swap node ids + edge targets (case-sensitive replace is safe since `ToolExecutionUpdate` contains neither token); write `.mmd` back as UTF-8 without BOM to avoid diff churn. Source: focused rename update for src/common/protocol (+ src/common, src/host/executor design/workflow).
- 2026-07-03: Enum variant rename dropping a redundant namespace prefix (`ExecutionSessionEvent::ExecutionEvoke/ExecutionQuery/ExecutionCancel/ExecutionKill/ExecutionStatus/ExecutionUpdate` -> `Evoke/Query/Cancel/Kill/Status/Update`), same line count, no line shift. Ground statuses against the reset baseline (HEAD was the design-reset commit right after the last tag) via `git diff HEAD -- <file>`, not against stale inherited annotations. Definition site (`execution.rs`): the owning enum element is `modified`; because an earlier in-window payload-type rename already touched two variant lines, the cumulative since-reset `modifiedLines` becomes the full contiguous renamed-variant span (lines 22-27), so widen the range rather than keep the earlier two singletons. Ripple call site (`src/agent/task/task.rs` event_loop match arm, lines 81-82): design-json-reset had left the Task element `unchanged` with STALE pre-tag annotations (segments 1/2 `modifiedLines`, segment 3 `addedLines:[63-176]`). Since the block existed at the reset baseline it is NOT added since tag, so when marking Task `modified` I cleared the stale segment annotations and set only `modifiedLines:[81-82]` â€” a `modified` element's line arrays are rendered, so they must reflect the true since-reset diff, unlike an `unchanged` element whose stale arrays are ignored (leave those verbatim). Ancestors: `src/agent/task` lists its own direct file `modified:["task.rs"]`; the descendant-only rollups `src/agent` (own files unchanged) use `modified:["."]`, and `src` was already `modified:["."]`. Hook only needs each ancestor `.design.json` present in the git change set. Workflow: only `src/common/protocol/.workflow.mmd` named old variant identifiers (edge labels) â€” host/agent/task diagrams referenced the TYPES `ExecutionStatus`/`ToolExecutionUpdate` and lowercase verbs, not variants, so they needed no edit. Source: focused variant-prefix rename for src/common/protocol/execution.rs + ripple in src/agent/task/task.rs.

- 2026-07-03: New tool modules + protocol type relocation refresh (common/tool, host/tool bin crate, host/executor folder). KEY: verify folder presence in the reset baseline before trusting caller "MODIFIED" language. Caller called host/executor "MODIFIED (rename ToolExecution->ExecutionRuntime)", but `git ls-tree HEAD src/host/executor/` was EMPTY (only a flat executor.rs existed at the tag) so the whole folder is `added:["."]` this window and ExecutionRuntime/ExecutionContext/Executor are all `added` elements, NOT a modified/rename â€” an intra-window rename before a tag stays `added`. When a public type MOVES modules (ToolPreview from common/protocol/execution.rs -> common/tool/tool.rs): delete the element from the old module .design.json, add it to the new owner, and in the referencing module keep the reference as a workflow edge into a common_tool subgraph node (ExecutionSessionEvent Preview{tools}) plus a purpose mention, not a local node/element. serde_json.rs re-export wrapper gaining `to_string` = external module `modified` + `modified:["serde_json.rs"]`, no element (re-export-only). For a feature-selected bin crate (marix-host-tool) where 5 [[bin]] share one tool_main.rs via required-features: Cargo.toml/tool_main.rs/lib.rs/mod.rs are manifest/entry/wiring (not elements); model ToolProgram (trait) + run (function) as host/tool elements, and each unit-struct native tool (ReadFile/WriteFile/ListDirectory/SearchText/ShellExecute) as a struct element with three segments (unit struct decl, inherent NAME/DESCRIPTION/*_SCHEMA const impl, and `impl ToolProgram`). Ground cumulative modifiedLines on `git diff HEAD` (reset baseline): protocol enum/struct renames + ToolExecutionUpdate->ExecutionUpdate land as modifiedLines on just the renamed declaration/variant lines (ExecutionSessionEvent 16-21, the four decl lines singly) while ExecutionSignature stays `unchanged` despite a +1 position shift from the added `use crate::tool::ToolPreview;` import. Hook check confirmed: every changed non-dot source file (incl. untracked new folders) had its full ancestor .design.json chain present in the git change set (untracked new .design.json count via `git ls-files --others`), 0 missing -> allow. Banned-token scan for `ToolExecution` across all .design.json+.workflow.mmd returned 0; `ToolProgram` survives only in the new host/tool tree. Source: ensure-deveopment-design refresh for src/common/tool, src/common/protocol, src/common/external, src/common, src/host/executor, src/host/tool tree, src/host, src.

- 2026-07-03: Intra-window structural churn inside already-`added` folders (host/tool: `runner.rs` deleted + its `run` moved into `tool_main.rs` as a private `fn run<T: ToolProgram>`; host/executor: `execution.rs`/`execution_context.rs` moved into a new `execution/` child module). Verified via `git ls-tree HEAD -- src/host/tool` and `src/host/executor` = EMPTY and `git status` = `??` (both folders untracked/new this window). So they STAY `added:["."]`, NOT `modified`, and files created-then-moved/deleted within the window are simply ABSENT â€” do NOT add `runner.rs`/`execution.rs` to a `deleted` array (they never existed at the tag), per the added-module-cannot-list-deletions rule. The caller's literal "mark module modified / runner.rs deleted" language was overridden by the tag-baseline check. Concrete edits: (1) host/tool/.design.json â€” delete the `run` function element (it moved into a bin entry, and bins have no elements), keep `ToolProgram`, rewrite purpose to say tool_main.rs `main` drives `run<T: ToolProgram>`; the per-file `#[cfg(feature=...)] pub use self::X as SelectedTool;` selectors are cfg-gated re-export WIRING, not elements, and the native tool structs (ReadFile/WriteFile/ListDirectory/SearchText/ShellExecute) are unchanged in shape so native/*/.design.json need no element edits. (2) host/executor/.design.json â€” remove the `ExecutionRuntime`/`ExecutionContext` elements (moved down), keep `Executor`, add the `execution` childModule (no changeStatus), refresh purpose. (3) Create host/executor/execution/.design.json as a NEW module `added:["."]` with `ExecutionRuntime`(execution.rs 8-13 struct,15-27 impl) + `ExecutionContext`(execution_context.rs 3-6 struct,8-12 impl), source_depth 4 (src=1..execution=4) â€” same line ranges as before since content moved verbatim, only sourcePaths change to `.../execution/...`. (4) The only real tag-relative deletion lives at the PARENT: host/.design.json keeps `deleted:["executor.rs"]` (flat file that WAS at the tag) + `modified:["lib.rs"]` (re-export dropped ToolExecution/ToolProgram) at module `modified`; only soften the host/tool childModule purpose that said "run drives ..." to "tool_main.rs bin entry drives ...". src rollup stays `modified:["."]`, no content edit needed (already in change set, no stale run/runner/execution.rs reference). Workflows: host/tool remove `runner_rs` subgraph, add a `tool_main.rs bin entry` node (main -> run) plus a cfg-selected `SelectedTool` node; host/executor regroup ExecutionRuntime/ExecutionContext under an `execution child module` subgraph; create execution/.workflow.mmd; the host rollup workflow grouping them under "executor module" stays valid. Guarded with a banned-token scan (`runner`, old `src/host/executor/execution.rs`/`execution_context.rs` paths, `"name": "run"`) = 0, an automated codeSegment boundary check (every start lands on `pub struct`/`pub trait`/`impl`, every end on `}`), and BOM-free `.mmd` writes. Hook auto-allows (development-designer writes only dot files) but ancestor chain is present regardless. Source: host/tool runner->tool_main run move + host/executor execution/ child-module split.

- 2026-07-03: ExecutionRuntime/ExecutionContext behavior fill-in (host/executor/execution/). BASELINE CHECK overturned the caller's "new within the current tag window" note: `git ls-tree <lasttag> src/host/executor/execution/` LISTED all files (folder existed AT the tag with module+elements `added`) and `git log <lasttag>..HEAD` showed the reset commit ("Reset design metadata changeStatus after ... tag") already ran, so HEAD's committed .design.json is `unchanged`. Therefore this window's first `git diff HEAD` edits are `modified`, NOT `added` â€” the caller's own conditional ("if elements were `added` at the reset baseline, keep added") did not apply because reset had flipped them to `unchanged`. Always run the ls-tree + reset-log pair before choosing added vs modified on caller instruction. NOTE the reset commit only flips changeStatus->unchanged and drops top-level status arrays; it LEAVES stale full-range addedLines on every segment. When marking modified this window, RECOMPUTE highlights from git diff HEAD and clear the fossil full-range addedLines on segments that only shifted position (ExecutionRuntime struct 8-13 -> 11-16 became addedLines:[]/modifiedLines:[] because its fields were untouched; only the import expansion above pushed it down). Panic-stub -> implemented method mapping: a single-line signature expanded to multi-line = first line (`pub fn new(`) modifiedLines + the split params/`) -> Self {`/body lines addedLines; a one-line panic body replaced by a real body = first new body line modifiedLines + the rest addedLines (sender line36 mod; run line40 mod + 41-49 added). A brand-new `// -- Private -- //` `impl ExecutionRuntime { event_loop/spawn_execution/emit_* }` block is a fully-added THIRD codeSegment (55-115) on the existing struct element, not a new element (private assoc fns are not elements). ExecutionContext struct gained 3 fields: addedLines on just the inserted field lines ([7-8],[10]), signature/agent_tx untouched. HOOK: host/Cargo.toml and src/Cargo.lock both PASS Test-NonDotSourcePath (start with src/, no dot segment, not tests), so host/Cargo.toml forces src/host + src .design.json updates; note the added `tokio` dep in module purpose (manifests are not elements/status entries). Ancestors whose own tracked source (executor.rs still panic stubs, session.rs, lib.rs) did not change stay module `modified` + `modified:["."]` with source-backed purpose/childModule edits describing the implemented execution runtime. Verified all 4 ancestor .design.json appear in `git diff --name-only` (hook allow) and every codeSegment/highlight range dumped from source matches intended text. Source: ensure-deveopment-design fill-in for src/host/executor/execution, src/host/executor, src/host, src.

- 2026-07-04: `run()`-merged-into-`new()` consolidation across three runtime types (Task in src/agent/task/task.rs, ExecutionRuntime in src/host/executor/execution/execution.rs, Session in src/agent/session/session.rs) + ExecutionParameterPackage `task_id: TaskId`->`signature: ExecutionSignature` (src/common/protocol/execution.rs) + ExecutionContext dropping its `signature` field (now `parameters.signature`). Grounded every changeStatus/range/highlight in `git diff HEAD` (HEAD=reset commit 4aead19, all changes cumulative-uncommitted since the last tag; the diff overrides caller prose). KEY TRAPS/LESSONS: (1) ONE-GAP ANCESTOR FIX â€” the worktree already had most .design.json/.workflow.mmd as `M` from an earlier abandoned run, but `src/common/protocol/.design.json` was NOT in the change set while `src/common/protocol/execution.rs` WAS changed, breaking that file's ancestor chain; a Python ancestor-chain checker (for each changed non-dot .rs, verify folder+every ancestor .design.json up to src is in `git diff --name-only HEAD`) caught the single gap. ALWAYS run that checker at the end. (2) `run` REMOVAL LEAVES NO LINE â€” dropping a public `run(&mut self)` method and struct fields (`task_rx`/`execution_rx`, `Option<JoinHandle>`->`JoinHandle`) are deletions with no current line to highlight; the surviving changed field line (e.g. `worker: JoinHandle<()>`) gets modifiedLines, the deletion contributes nothing. `new` now spawning the worker = the split params + new spawn/body lines are addedLines, the `pub fn new(` line itself is modifiedLines (signature changed). A brand-new `// -- Private -- //` `impl` block (event_loop etc.) is a fully-added codeSegment on the existing struct element (private assoc fns are not elements). (3) STUB->IMPL vs SHIFT â€” execution.rs/execution_context.rs were PANIC STUBS at HEAD, so relative to HEAD they are stub->full-impl `modified` (folder existed at last tag with elements originally `added`, reset flipped to `unchanged`); recompute all highlights, don't carry fossil full-range arrays. ExecutionContext struct field swap: `signature`->`tool` line modifiedLines[{4,4}], inserted `parameters` line addedLines[{5,5}]; new() body stub->real = modifiedLines on `pub fn new(` + addedLines on split params/body. (4) SINGLE-LINE FIELD SWAP â€” ExecutionParameterPackage `task_id`->`signature` is one line: element `modified`, modifiedLines[{46,46}] only (other fields prompt/tool_request/user_options + other execution.rs elements stay `unchanged` with fossil arrays left verbatim â€” renderer ignores unchanged arrays). (5) WORKFLOW EDGE REWIRES â€” ExecutionContext no longer holds signature so its `-->|"signature"| ExecutionSignature` edge moves onto ExecutionParameterPackage (or becomes `parameters.signature` where the context still reaches it); Task/Session/ExecutionRuntime workflow comments say "new spawns worker(s), no separate run"; `ExecutionParameterPackage -->|"task_id"| TaskId` becomes `-->|"signature"| ExecutionSignature`. (6) STALE-SCAN FALSE POSITIVES â€” after edits, `task_id` survives legitimately inside prose describing ExecutionSignature's OWN fields (`ExecutionSignature { task_id, exe_id, name }`) and the retained `ExecutionSignature -->|"task_id"| TaskId` edge; `execution_rx` survives as behavior text ("loops execution_rx handling Cancel->Canceled") since it's the receiver local in event_loop, not a struct field claim â€” none are stale. Validation battery: all 20 .design.json parse; all codeSegment ranges in-bounds and addedLines/modifiedLines within their segment; every highlighted line dumped from source matched intended text (verified ExecutionRuntime struct{14}/new{18,37,19-33}/private{43-106} and ExecutionContext{4,5,10,11-19} line-by-line vs `git diff HEAD`); all 6 edited .mmd valid Mermaid (balanced subgraph/end, `flowchart` header) and BOM-free (edit tool preserves encoding); ancestor chain satisfied for all 8 changed source files. Source: ensure-deveopment-design refresh for src/common/protocol, src/common, src/agent/task, src/agent/session, src/agent, src/host/executor/execution, src/host/executor, src/host, src.

- 2026-07-03: Network channel remoc `rch::base`->`rch::mpsc` synchronous-send refresh (per-send tokio runtimes removed; sends now `try_send` via `std::sync::Mutex`). SIX baseline traps, all resolved by `git diff HEAD` against the reset commit, NOT caller prose: (1) STALE INTERMEDIATE WORKING-TREE DESIGN â€” the host execution .design.json/.workflow.mmd were already `M` in the worktree from an earlier abandoned run that described a "per-execution tokio runtime" the current source never keeps; do NOT trust worktree design content, recompute every host segment against actual current source. ## 2026-07-03 â€” Protocol rename + Executor::evoke metadata wave

- Baseline HEAD may be a *reset commit* (design-json-reset run): `git diff HEAD` then shows the **cumulative** uncommitted diff across MULTIPLE change waves, not just the wave described in the current request. Set element/module `changeStatus` from the full cumulative diff, but only touch the entries the request's wave actually affects; leave prior-wave entries as they already are.
- The `design-json-reset` skill resets `changeStatus`->"unchanged" and drops top-level status arrays, but does NOT clear `addedLines`/`modifiedLines`. So baseline files carry pre-reset "fossil" highlights on otherwise-unchanged elements. When marking an element changed for the current wave, clear its fossils and set precise git-diff-HEAD ranges; leave untouched elements' fossils alone to minimize churn.
- **Derive-insertion line shift gotcha**: adding `#[derive(Clone)]` above a struct shifts every subsequent line in the file by +1. When a prior run wrote codeSegments BEFORE such an insert, ALL later segments/highlights in that file must be re-based (+1) against current source, not just the changed item. Always recompute line ranges from the *current* source, never trust pre-existing numbers after an insertion/deletion.
- A pure rename (`ExecutionParameterPackage`->`ExecutionRequest`) => element `changeStatus: "renamed"` with `modifiedLines` on the name line (renamed elements may carry modifiedLines per the schema example). A `SessionEvent::Evoke(ExecutionRequest)` variant line whose characters are textually unchanged stays "unchanged" even though the carried type was renamed.
- Rename verification: after propagating, `grep` the old type name across all `.design.json`/`.workflow.mmd` under src/ and expect ZERO hits â€” including historical prose notes. Record the rename via the element's `renamed` status, not by leaving the dead type name in purpose text.
- Element-level status must be per-definition: a file listed in a folder's top-level `modified` array does NOT make every element in that file "modified". Only elements whose own definition/codeSegments changed get a changed status.
- Crate-root/workspace-root `.workflow.mmd` files are element-agnostic (module/crate boxes only), so element renames and single-method implementations usually need NO change there â€” report them as intentionally unchanged.

- 2026-07-04: Agent-crate "move methods onto self" refactor design update. When a `*_context.rs`/`*Context` is renamed to `*_state.rs`/`*State` AND absorbs worker/step logic moved out of the sibling `session.rs`/`task.rs`, git reports the new file as untracked (pure delete of the old file, no rename detected). Design-metadata judgment: represent it as top-level `renamed: ["*_state.rs"]` (not added+deleted) because the struct keeps identical fields (only channel type params SessionEvent->SessionMessage change) and the file rename is deliberate; note old->new name + absorbed logic in prose. Within that renamed file, mark the carried-over struct/new() lines as modifiedLines (name + retyped fields) and the moved-in methods as addedLines (they had no counterpart in the predecessor file). The now-thin session.rs/task.rs are `modified` (struct context type change, new() signature, thinned bodies, added package_message/SOURCE_NAME). mod.rs wiring changes are listed in the `modified` array but contribute no elements.
- 2026-07-04: pub(super) methods that move onto the shared state (spawn_client_worker/spawn_host_worker on SessionState; event_loop/run_step on TaskState) ARE elements (type "function"), with changeStatus "added" when newly exposed at that location. Fully-private neighbors (run_client_worker/run_host_worker/create_task/route_session_event/route_task_event/parse_address; initial_step/execute_model_step/emit_status/send_event) are folded as extra codeSegments on the owning struct element, not separate elements.
- 2026-07-04: package_message is NOT modeled as its own element anywhere in this repo (HostSession folds it in); keep Session's associated fns (new, package_message) folded into the Session struct element's codeSegments for consistency.
- 2026-07-04: design-json-reset preserves codeSegments (including addedLines/modifiedLines) verbatim and only resets changeStatus->unchanged + strips top-level status arrays + strips childModules changeStatus. So "unchanged" sibling elements (step.rs/execution.rs) legitimately keep historical addedLines after a reset; preserve them verbatim and do not clear them.

- 2026-07-05: CORRECTION superseding the two 2026-07-04 lessons above ("pub(super) methods ARE elements" and "package_message is NOT its own element") â€” the agent state/behavior split was reorganized again and those earlier calls were WRONG per the user. Current truth: session_state.rs/task_state.rs (renamed from session_context.rs/task_context.rs) are PURE DATA structs â€” only `pub struct SessionState{tasks,client_tx,client_rx,host_tx,host_rx}`+`pub fn new()` and `pub struct TaskState{signature,model_backend,client_tx,host_tx,executions,steps}`+`pub fn new()`. Every one is a fully-private `fn` (no pub/pub(crate)/pub(super)) so NONE are elements â€” spawn_*/run_*/create_task/route_*/event_loop/run_step/initial_step/execute_model_step/emit_status/send_event/build_model_backend/parse_address are represented ONLY as an extra codeSegment on the owning struct element (private impl SessionState folds onto SessionState, impl TaskState onto TaskState, build_model_backend onto Task). Rollup purposes (session/task childModule in agent/.design.json, agent childModule in src/.design.json, both module.purpose) must state behavior lives in session.rs/task.rs as private impls on the state types â€” NOT 'SessionState owns spawn/run/create/route', NOT 'pub(super)', NOT 'thin ... owns methods'; keep 'renamed from *_context.rs' prose.
